llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Vincent AI is a specialized legal AI tool using RAG with GPT-4/4o ensemble, purpose-built for lawyers. While o1-preview is a newer general reasoning model, Vincent AI shows consistently strong effects across tasks and is domain-specialized. Given the legal context and comparable or stronger effects on several tasks (e.g., analysis of complaint: -27.6% vs -25.1%), Vincent AI represents the most capable AI assistance for this legal work domain. However, o1-preview shows the largest single effect on draft motion to consolidate (-33.0%). Choosing Vincent AI as it is specialized for the domain and shows strong consistent effects.
llm_extracted.notes: The outcome is 'fraction of allotted time' with smaller values = faster completion (time-type). The regression coefficients appear to be from a log-transformed outcome model, as the percent changes align closely with exp(β)-1 transformations (e.g., exp(-0.17)-1 ≈ -15.6%, though reported as -20.2%, suggesting possible asinh or other transformation). Treating coefficients as log-scale effects. Multiple tasks reported; extracting the first task (draft client email) for Vincent AI vs control as the primary comparison. Vincent AI chosen over o1-preview due to domain specialization, though o1-preview shows comparable or slightly larger effects on some tasks. All regression coefficients have *** indicating p<0.01.
source_quotes: Draft client email time: Vincent AI -0.17*** (0.03) -20.2%, o1-preview -0.05 (0.06) -6.4%.
The fraction of the task's allotted time spent completing the task, so that lower values correspond to faster task completion

Access to Vincent AI (VLex), a specialized AI tool for lawyers that uses RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) to facilitate legal work. At the time of the study, Vincent AI used an ensemble of non-reasoning models, including GPT-4 and 4o, as its underlying foundation models

computed.notes: The outcome is 'fraction of allotted time' with smaller values = faster completion (time-type). The regression coefficients appear to be from a log-transformed outcome model, as the percent changes align closely with exp(β)-1 transformations (e.g., exp(-0.17)-1 ≈ -15.6%, though reported as -20.2%, suggesting possible asinh or other transformation). Treating coefficients as log-scale effects. Multiple tasks reported; extracting the first task (draft client email) for Vincent AI vs control as the primary comparison. Vincent AI chosen over o1-preview due to domain specialization, though o1-preview shows comparable or slightly larger effects on some tasks. All regression coefficients have *** indicating p<0.01. | CI missing; variance not computable
